2026-05-16.night_summary: 1-1 night, +0.30u. The PHI @ PIT under 8.5 was the easy money — Cristopher Sánchez threw a complete-game shutout, striking out a career-high 13 while Bubba Chandler walked four in three innings for Pittsburgh, and the 6-0 final never sniffed the number. The MIA @ TB under 7.5 is the one that stings, but not because the read was wrong — through nine innings it was a 2-2 ballgame, exactly what the thesis called for, with the under alive by 3.5 runs. Then Hunter Bigge and the Rays' bullpen imploded in the 10th, surrendering 8 runs on 6 hits including a Javier Sanoja double that plated three, turning a clean under into a 10-5 final. Variance got us, not a bad model — that game had no business going over. Ladder sits at 1 of 10 — we're on the board, climbing.

2026-05-17.night_summary: 2-1 night, +1.03u. The Mets ML came through the hard way: New York trailed by four before Tyrone Taylor tied it with a homer and Carson Benge walked it off in the 10th for a 7-6 win. The SD @ SEA under was a model miss — Kirby got torched for six runs in 5.2 innings, including two homers off Gavin Sheets, dropping to a 10.50 ERA in three career starts against San Diego; the red flag was flagged and underweighted, and we own that one. Ladder sits at 2 of 10 — climbing.

2026-05-18.night_summary: 0-2 night, -2.50u — a rough one, but the stories behind the losses are different. The BAL @ TB under (ladder pick) was a model miss, plain and simple: Rogers sent 10 batters to the plate in a five-run second inning, then Tampa Bay batted around again in a four-run sixth capped by Caminero's three-run homer, and the final was 16-6 — more than double the 7.5 line before the fifth inning was over. We got that one wrong; a 1.54 WHIP screamed big-inning risk and we discounted it. The SA @ OKC pick is a different story — Thunder -6.5 was alive and competitive through regulation, with OKC clawing back from a 10-point deficit and the lead changing hands twice in the final two minutes, before Wembanyama's logo-range three in the first OT and a pair of sealing dunks in double OT finished it 122-115. Variance got us there, not a bad read. Ladder resets to 0 of 10 — we start climbing again tomorrow.

2026-05-19.night_summary: 1-1 night, -0.25u. The Reds were the bright spot — Cincinnati went into Philly and took care of business 4-1 at +125, a clean win that paid. The ladder pick is the one that stings, and it deserves an honest explanation: the Mets were up 5-0 before the Nationals had even recorded two outs in the 2nd, then James Wood lofted a first-pitch sweeper to deep left where debuting rookie Nick Morabito — in his first MLB at-bat in the field — couldn't hang on, and the ball caromed into center for the first inside-the-park grand slam in the majors since 2022. Washington scored three more in the 3rd and never looked back, winning 9-6. That is pure variance — a once-in-years defensive freak play on a ball that had no business leaving the infield. We got the read right; the baseball gods had other plans. Ladder resets at 0 of 10 — we climb again tomorrow.

2026-05-20.night_summary: 2-1 night, +0.84u, but the ladder took a hit. The two non-ladder unders were clean — TOR @ NYY finished 2-1 with Schlittler and Yesavage both dealing, and HOU @ MIN was a quiet 4-1 with the Astros' offense going nowhere. Both unders cleared without drama. The ladder loss is on us — ATL @ MIA under 7.0 was a model miss. Chris Sale was everything we needed: 7 IP, 1 ER, 8 K. But Janson Junk was a disaster waiting to happen, and we didn't weight it enough — he allowed a career-high 8 runs, including Austin Riley's 3-run shot in the 2nd and Dominic Smith's 3-run blast in the 6th, and couldn't get out of the fifth. The 9-1 final wasn't bad luck; Junk's May ERA was the tell we underweighted. Ladder resets at 0 of 10 — back to the bottom, starting fresh.

2026-05-21.night_summary: 1-0 night, +0.95u. The game did get a little spicy late: Colorado tied it at 1 in the eighth on a bases-loaded HBP, and Corbin Carroll walked it off with a two-out single in the ninth to make it 2-1 — but that's still well clear of 9.5. Clean win, no sweat after the first pitch. Ladder is at 1 of 10 — back on the board and climbing.

2026-05-22.night_summary: 1-0 night, +1.36u. Taillon gave up four but the game was never in danger of going over. Straightforward read, straightforward result. Ladder is at 2 of 10 — climbing.

2026-05-23.night_summary: 2-1 night, +1.75u. The NBA loss stings: we backed Cleveland -2.5 at home in a must-win elimination spot and the Knicks led wire-to-wire, jumping out 9-1 inside two minutes and never looking back; Landry Shamet buried it with three threes in 99 seconds in the fourth to push it to 105-94. We got that one wrong — the home urgency narrative ran straight into a historically dominant road team. Ladder is at 3 of 10 — climbing.

2026-05-24.night_summary: 1-1 night, +0.30u — but both pick scores in the input were misattributed from the day before, so let's set the record straight. 2026-05-25.night_summary: 2-3 night, -1.76u — a rough Memorial Day. The PHI @ SD under (3-0) and MIL -1.5 over STL (5-1) both cashed cleanly, but the losses were the story. The Cubs ladder pick was similarly snakebitten — Ben Brown was excellent through six, Michael Busch tied it 1-1 in the 5th, and the game turned on a single Trent Thornton cutter that Henry Davis — hitting .105 in May — sent over the right-field fence in the 7th for a 2-1 final. The Toronto run-line was a model miss: Yesavage got tagged for a career-high five runs, Vladdy sat with an elbow issue, and Junk dealt. We got that one wrong. The two player props (Marte, Witt) and the White Sox ML were model errors — wrong-pitcher mismatch, recency-blind pitcher evaluation, and ignoring career splits against a specific opponent, respectively — and we'll be tightening those filters. The two wins that carried it: Cam Schlittler punched out 8 in NYY @ TB (cleared 5.5 with room to spare) and the Yankees backed him up with a clean 5-1 win, notching the ladder's fourth straight. ARI @ SD under 8.5 was never in doubt at 8-0 final. The losses stung differently — TOR @ SF was a model miss, plain and simple: the under leaned on Gausman as a stabilizing arm while his last-six ERA and velocity decline were both screaming red flags; he was shelled for a 5-run sixth and the game was 8-1 before the ninth. PHI @ KC was pure variance — Sánchez, a Cy Young candidate with a 2.00 ERA, had the worst start of his career (9 ER, 3 HR in 3⅓ innings against the second-worst team in baseball); that one wasn't a bad read, it was a one-in-a-hundred game. Ladder sits at 4 of 10 — climbing.

2026-07-07.night_summary: 2-3-1 night, -0.45u — a rough one to absorb. The bright spots were real: Wheeler went off for 14 Ks across seven innings in the PHI 4-1 win over CIN, cashing the Over 7.5 at +120 and covering the -1.5 with room to spare. Skubal was a clean MODEL loss — he struck out the side in the first and finished with 9 Ks on 96 pitches, exposing the flaw in the pitch-count-caps-strikeouts thesis. The SEA/MIA Under was alive through seven before a chaotic Mariners eighth and a pinch-hit Marlins homer made it 6-5 — variance, not a bad read. The LAA/TEX Under was a MODEL miss from the jump: deGrom gave up two in the first, and Osuna's three-run shot capped a five-run Texas eighth to make it 8-3, busting the total by four. Ladder holds at 5 of 10 — still climbing.

2026-07-08.night_summary: 2-3 night, -1.55u — two clean wins buried under a Logan Webb disaster and a Chase Burns walk-fest. The NYY @ TB under (ladder) was the best ticket of the night: Shane McClanahan tossed 6.1 scoreless, Jonathan Aranda drove in all three Rays runs, and New York was shut out 3-0 — well inside the 7. MIL @ STL under was equally tidy at 6-1. The damage came from Oracle Park: Webb got lit for 10 runs including Okamoto's grand slam and homers from Guerrero Jr. and Springer, torching both the Giants ML and the TOR @ SF under in one implosion — model errors, both, for leaning on Webb's season ERA while ignoring his 7-ER meltdown the start prior. Burns was variance — he walked a career-high six in five innings against a Phillies lineup that refused to chase, finishing with just two strikeouts; the read was right, the approach wasn't. Ladder sits at 6 of 10 — keep climbing.

2026-07-09.night_summary: Rough one — 2-4 night, -2.82u, with the ladder pick doing the most damage. The two bright spots were real: Gavin Williams was filthy in Minneapolis, retiring the first 13 Twins he faced before finishing with 11 strikeouts over seven innings in a clean 5-2 CLE win, and the Guardians ML cashed alongside it at +0.87u. The SEA @ MIA under (ladder) was the gut punch — Bryce Miller walked four and surrendered nine hits in five innings against a Miami offense that had gone 19-7 over its last 26 games, a hot-streak signal the model explicitly dismissed; the game was effectively over after an O. Lopez-keyed four-run frame made it 6-1, and the 8-4 final blew past the total before the fifth was complete — that one's on the model, not variance. The PHI -1.5 and Schwarber prop losses were cleaner variance: Singer was dominant in a 1-0 pitcher's duel, and the LAA @ TEX under was alive at 6-1 through six before Detmers got bombed for three homers in a historically anomalous outing and a same-day Langford IL activation walked it off in the ninth. Ladder resets at 0 of 10 — back to the bottom, start climbing.

2026-07-10.night_summary: 3-2 night, +0.94u. The two unders that mattered most both cashed clean — ATL @ STL finished 1-2 (well inside 8.0) and the ladder pick, CLE @ MIA, closed 3-2 in nine with Parker Messick dealing and the Marlins never threatening the total after the 7th. COL @ SF chipped in a quiet 4-3 cover of the 8.0 as well. The Imanaga K prop was a process loss, plain and simple — we built the thesis around Hunter Greene being compromised, but he was fully healthy and put up one of the best starts of his career: 12 strikeouts in 7 scoreless innings against the Cubs, leaving Imanaga with just 5 Ks in 5 innings and no path to the over. The BOS @ NYM under was the same story — we leaned on Boston's season R/G and discounted McLean's walk problem against a disciplined lineup; Red Sox put up 6 and covered the 7.5 with room. Two model misses, owned. Ladder is at 1 of 10 — back on the board, climbing.

2026-07-11.night_summary: 3-2-1 night, +1.06u. The ladder pick carried the card — Cristopher Sanchez and Casey Mize combined to keep PHI @ DET at 4-2, well inside the 7.5, and that's two straight for the climb. Giants ML was clean too, SF handled Colorado 4-2 with no drama, and Elly delivered exactly 2 total bases in CHC @ CIN to cash the prop. The SEA @ TB under pushed at exactly 7 — fine, move on. The two losses were model errors, not variance: Joe Ryan finished with 6 Ks against the Angels when the projected total was essentially a coin flip at 7.5, and the Dodgers -1.5 got torched 9-3 by Arizona — Ohtani was a DH nursing a bad knee, the Dodgers ran a bullpen game, and Eduardo Rodriguez was the one who looked like the ace. We got those wrong. Ladder is at 2 of 10 — climbing.

2026-07-12.night_summary: Rough night — 2-4, down roughly -2.92u. The ladder pick carried the evening: Zack Wheeler was dominant against Detroit, and PHI 5 · DET 0 cleared the Under 7 with room to spare, pushing the streak to 3. The Cubs ML also cashed cleanly at CHC 8 · CIN 4. The SEA @ TB Under 7.5 was the gut-punch — Yandy Díaz drilled a 107.2 mph comebacker off Hancock's bare hand in the first inning, and though Hancock gutted out the first and signaled out in the second, Seattle was suddenly running a bullpen game; Arozarena's three-run shot made it 7-0 and the 8-2 final wasn't close. Pure black swan — no model prices pitch one. The TOR @ SD Under died in the 8th on a Machado tie-ball and a Ty France walkoff at 5-4, Boyd's 3 Ks was a model miss we own, and Bradley's 6 Ks — one short at 6.5 — was clean variance. Ladder sits at 3 of 10 — still climbing.

2026-07-16.night_summary: 2-2 night, -0.90u — the props split clean and the game bets went the wrong way. Harper and Schwarber both cashed their TB overs at +105, each finishing with 2 total bases, easy money. The over 9.5 was a model miss — we got the Nola HR-prone thesis right (three solo shots off him), but Christian Scott walked nobody and struck out seven, the Phillies offense went ice cold, and a 4-1 final never sniffed 9.5. We got that one wrong. The Soto TB ladder pick is a different story: variance killed it, not the read. Soto was 1-for-1 with three walks before being pulled in the 8th with left calf soreness — the Mets don't expect him to miss time, but his night was done at 1 total base with no chance to accumulate. Unforeseeable, clean variance. Ladder resets to 0 of 10 — back to the bottom, start climbing.

2026-07-17.night_summary: 2-1-1 night, +1.04u. Atlanta was the story — the Braves didn't just cover -1.5, they buried Texas 15-1, and the 1.5u bet at +106 was the night's best earner at +1.59u. The MIA @ MIL under was quiet and clean, a 2-1 final that never sniffed 8. The Yankees ML stings because the read was right: Cole was dominant through six, New York led 1-0, and the loss came down to one hung 2-2 slider to Muncy in the seventh — a two-run shot that flipped it 2-1 Dodgers for good. Pure variance, not a bad ticket. The ladder pick, SF @ SEA under 7, pushed exactly on a 7-0 final — no harm, no gain, streak stays at 0 of 10.

2026-07-18.night_summary: 2-3-1 night, -2.98u — a rough one, and the losses deserve honest explanations. The bright spots: Tatis went off for 4 total bases in SD @ KC (WIN, +0.83u) and Milwaukee held Miami 2-1 in a tight one (WIN, +0.68u). The CIN @ COL over (1.5u) was a DATA loss — the entire thesis was built around Sugano and Lowder starting; Brady Singer went seven strong and Gabriel Hughes held Cincy to two runs, and the final 7-2 never had a chance at 12.5. The ladder pick, Tigers -1.5, is the one that stings: Detroit won 2-1, directionally correct, but Hao-Yu Lee's two-run double in the top of the 9th was the only offense all game — a 1-run margin when we needed 2, pure variance, not a bad read. Ladder resets to 0 of 10 — we start the climb again tomorrow.
